section.title: AI and the Global South
section.intro: Who builds AI tools, who owns the training data, and what that means for health systems that depend on both.

resources.title: EVAH Initiative: $60M for Locally-Led AI Evaluation in LMICs
resources.source: Wellcome Trust, Gates Foundation, Novo Nordisk Foundation
resources.description: $60M initiative funding locally-led AI evaluation capacity in LMICs. Administered by J-PAL. Only 4 of 86 AI health RCTs have been conducted in LMICs.

resources.title: America First, Africa Last? Health Data Deals
resources.description: Documents health data extraction from Kenya, Rwanda, and Uganda through partnership agreements that give US/European institutions access with minimal reciprocal benefit.

resources.title: AI, Digital Colonialism, and Africa's Future
resources.description: 98% of AI research originates from wealthy institutions. Maps the structural inequality in AI development, not just deployment.

resources.title: Africa Declares AI Strategic Priority
resources.description: Phase 1 implementation (2025–2026) of the AU Continental AI Strategy, building domestic capacity for AI development, evaluation, and regulation.

resources.title: AI for Africa, by Africa
resources.description: UNESCO program to train 15,000 civil servants, 5,000 judicial personnel, and 1,500 researchers across Africa in AI governance.

resources.title: Digital Sovereignty in Africa
resources.source: Policy Center for the New South
resources.description: Maps infrastructure dependencies: undersea cables financed by tech giants, cloud services concentrated in a few companies, training data flowing out of the continent.

resources.title: Data Colonialism and the Emerging Voice of the Global South
resources.author: Anita Gurumurthy & Nandini Chami
resources.description: Frames AI data extraction from the Global South as a structural continuation of colonial resource flows.

resources.title: Ubuntu as an Ethical Framework for AI Governance
resources.author: Sabelo Mhlambi
resources.description: Proposes Ubuntu – relational personhood, mutual obligation – as a foundation for AI governance that doesn't begin from Western liberal individualism.

resources.title: The Costs of Connection
resources.author: Nick Couldry & Ulises Mejias
resources.description: Academic treatment of data colonialism as an analytical framework. The framework has become standard vocabulary for understanding AI adoption in contexts of structural inequality.

callout.title: The sovereign AI movement
callout.body: Countries are building domestic AI capacity rather than depending on US and Chinese models. The AU Continental AI Strategy entered Phase 1 implementation in 2025. France, India, the UAE, and several AU member states have active strategies.
